summary: Validated the new _headers file emitted by build-static.js for Cloudflare Pages CDN cache policy. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_headers_file.py (32 new checks: syntax, per-block Cache-Control values, Content-Type on sitemap/robots, expected 15 blocks). Also ran the previous 16-check _redirects regression suite. All 48 tests pass. Preview host spot-check on /cr1 confirms title 'CR1 Hot Tub — 5-Person Rectangular Spa | California Cooperage' and Product offers JSON-LD still present.
action_items: User must redeploy /app/frontend/_headers + /app/frontend/_redirects to Cloudflare Pages for the production cache policy to take effect (out-of-environment step).
critical_code_review_comments: build-static.js _headers emitter correctly follows Cloudflare Pages spec — path lines start with /, header lines indented with exactly 2 spaces, blank lines separate blocks, comments are top-of-block only.
Emitter is ROUTES-derived: every route with a slug automatically gets both /<slug> and /<slug>/ blocks — so future new slugs will get cache policy for free (same DRY pattern as _redirects).

One minor style observation (not a bug): the /*.html glob would in theory cover /cr1/index.html etc., so the per-slug short-cache blocks are strictly redundant with /*.html. However, Cloudflare Pages evaluates URL patterns against the request URL (which is /cr1, NOT /cr1/index.html) — so /*.html would NOT match /cr1. The extensionless per-slug blocks are therefore correct and necessary.
Long-cache immutable is applied only to /cc-assets/*, correctly scoped. Content-Type on /sitemap.xml and /robots.txt is correctly set with explicit charset=utf-8.

Note: production URL (https://californiacooperagehottubs.com) cannot be tested from this environment — verification of the actual CDN cache-control response headers requires the user to redeploy and probe the production host.
seed_data_creation: Ran `node build-static.js` in /app/frontend which produced /app/frontend/_headers (15 blocks), /app/frontend/_redirects (20 rules), sitemap.xml (6 urls), and 6 pre-rendered HTML files.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_headers_file.py auto-runs `node build-static.js` at module scope, then filesystem-parses /app/frontend/_headers into blocks (path + headers dict). It cross-checks each expected block's Cache-Control + Content-Type against the spec. Also runs regression against _redirects (still 20 rules, 301 before 200) and prerendered HTML offers block. test_redirects_file.py already existed from iteration_2. Both files combined form the regression suite going forward.

test_coverage_detail.per_slug_no_trailing_slash_short_cache: PASS on cr1, cr2, cr3, warranty, find-a-dealer
test_coverage_detail.per_slug_with_trailing_slash_short_cache: PASS on cr1/, cr2/, cr3/, warranty/, find-a-dealer/
test_coverage_detail.preview_spot_check_cr1: PASS — title contains 'CR1 Hot Tub' and offers JSON-LD present
